subject: FW: (no subject)
body:
From: Sean J. Lancaster [mailto:slancaster@bristolassociates.com] 
Sent: Tuesday, December 04, 2007 8:59 AM
To: lvjet@aol.com; Lesley Groff; Darren Indyke
Subject: RE: (no subject)

 

Larry I am copying the office and Darren and well

 

Please call me when you can about that offer.  

*         This is a dealer making the offer in a back to back.  I don't
like these transactions as I don't know the real purchase price. This is
supposed to be a Brazilian buyer. 

*         I think the sale rice should be higher than $12.5 given lead
times for a new one and our activity level. 

*         I think the deposit should be increased to $1 million at the
signing of the contract. 

*         I do think the $300K initial deposit is a bit low bit I am ok
with it. 

*         Ideally the really end used needs to be indentified  { think
you may need this for OFAC rules}

 

We go continue to get a lot of calls on the aircraft.  We do get push
back comments about the price but I always hear that no matter what the
market conditions are.  I think the buyer is likely paying $13.0 maybe
more for this aircraft.  Business Aircraft Leasing is openly referring
to the legal issues surrounding the seller as told to them by George
Reenstra and a Sikorsky salesman.  I immediately and repeatedly have
responded that is nonsense and complete irrelevant.  I do feel this may
be a real deal but I also feel we need Darren's usual thoroughness to be
certain.  

 

 

Best regards 

 

 

Sean J. Lancaster

Bristol Associates Inc.

1-540-665-5135 direct

1-202-682-4000 ext 1130 Main Office

1-540-533-8183 Mobile

1-540-301-0050 FAX

slancaster@bristolassociates.com

www.bristolassociates.com

From: Sean J. Lancaster [mailto:slancaster@bristolassociates.com]
Sent: Tuesday, December 04, 2007 8:59 AM
To: lvjet@aol.com; Lesley Groff; Darren Indyke
Subject: RE: (no subject)

 

Larry I am copying the office and Darren and well

 

Please call me when you can about that offer. 

·         This is a dealer making the offer in a back to back.  I don’t like these transactions as I don’t know the real purchase price. This is supposed to be a Brazilian buyer.

·         I think the sale rice should be higher than $12.5 given lead times for a new one and our activity level.

·         I think the deposit should be increased to $1 million at the signing of the contract.

·         I do think the $300K initial deposit is a bit low bit I am ok with it.

·         Ideally the really end used needs to be indentified  { think you may need this for OFAC rules}

 

We go continue to get a lot of calls on the aircraft.  We do get push back comments about the price but I always hear that no matter what the market conditions are.  I think the buyer is likely paying $13.0 maybe more for this aircraft.  Business Aircraft Leasing is openly referring to the legal issues surrounding the seller as told to them by George Reenstra and a Sikorsky salesman.  I immediately and repeatedly have responded that is nonsense and complete irrelevant.  I do feel this may be a real deal but I also feel we need Darren’s usual thoroughness to be certain. 

 

 

Best regards

 

 

Sean J. Lancaster
